author: @docink6175
text: 50c doesnt sound impressive all, 50c-122f does sound impressive though, it actually sound too warm for me to drink... interesting build though

author: @BillDeWitt
text:
I'm going to try just painting the bottom of the cup and putting "C" shaped contacts on the pad. Seems safer and easier. 
Right now I use a $9 coffee cup warmer from Amazon.

author: @htmagic
text: Luke, paint some thermochromatic paint or ink on the cup. When cold it is dark, as it heats up, it changes color or goes clear.

author: @SlyerFox666
text: I discovered inductive heating with a wireless phone charger and a metal disc on the back of my phone which attaches to a magnetic holder. It melted the disc into the back of the phone case while it was sat charging 😂. I wonder if you could do the same just put the mug on a cheapy wireless charger and a metal disc on the bottom of the cup or maybe inside at the bottom of the cup.

author: @karlmyers6518
text: With a fresh trim , comes a confident well presented video. I love this idea as I hate picking my cold coffee up and having to immediately spit it across the workshop lol

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: hahaha me too! I usually down it as quick as i can even though it feels like -10 degrees LOL! Thank you mate. 😁😁

text: video bars???????

author: @William_Hada
text: I really like this idea. Nice job!   11°C in the work area? Brrrrrr!!!! I see why you are wearing your winter coat indoors. LOL!

author: @jimmylimestone
text: Hi Luke: Will this self regulate or will it melt the cup?

author: @michaelcorbidge7914
text: Yes., we become inventive on aa number of things in the c o ld  and we see you are in a cold workshop . Once in USA i noticed that insulated mugs with flip tops were available. Useful . Tea cosies can be used to keep cup hot longer . A beanie makes a good cup cosie, several  beanies even better . I do in fact do this .

text: Camera seems to be on the blink but Thank you

text:
Will the ink wash of in time?
And can you put it in the dishwasher?

replies.author: @jimmylimestone
replies.text: All depends on the type of cover paint, Tempera or Acrylic.
